item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice! I love space SCPs, and this one was really good!

From what I understand, they stumbled into a pocket dimension with projective geometry?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got the twist pretty early, but this was pretty good. You've got a Kline for a Klein in the last offset, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is! I'm glad someone got that without reading it in the info bar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that Vietnam book The Things They Carried?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oops, good catch, thanks for that.

I probably will take your advice on the end note once I feel up to editing this again. There've been several people who thought the payoff for the mystery part of this thing wasn't satisfying enough, so if I can replace that ranty first paragraph with some hints as to why this thing exists, I think it'll make it a lot stronger.

I also do think I could do some more stuff with these characters and the Briareus Project in the future, so hopefully you haven't seen the last of 'em yet. Glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's amazing how frightening a small detail like "I could swear that Losev parted her hair on the other side." can be. (Also, there's a "Losov" in there.)

Fantastic character work. I was actually sad this was so short, I could have read a few more entries by this crew. I also have to say, I was a little miffed by the final note, but ending on a contemplation of "just what qualifies as a space skip" actually makes for an extra layer of interest. You might want to rewrite it with that as the focus. Either way, good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first half was rather dull. I'm glad I read on, but I do think this piece drags in the beginning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this because the characters didn't all flip out and die like what usually happens when an MTF goes inside an extradimensional thing. Most of them kept their cool, they solved the problem, and they escaped. Thank you for finally portraying the people who do these kinds of things as good at their jobs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: figured out about 2/3rds through the first off-set that their chirality hand changed, was proud of myself for figuring it out early. But didn't have the huge payoff I thought you were moving towards. No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the characterization, other than that the ending needs to be a bit stronger, a lot of buildup but the payoff isn't this wow moment we were waiting for. I can't stress enough that this is a strong article with parts to improve and you've done a very good job man!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Appreciate the feedback. Having as many characters as we did was ambitious, and we definitely could have done a better job with the execution.

As far as the crew not noticing the backwards writing goes, we tossed around quite a few explanations behind it. I don't believe that we ever agreed on one.

Either way, I appreciate the crit. Always good to know what we can improve on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appreciate the crit. What parts can we improve?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appreciate it, thanks for reading :)

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

I have very mixed feelings about this article. Some of it is pretty good, but then some of it really isn't something I enjoy. As far as the characters are concerned, none of them particularly interested me. Qi is my least favourite character in there, being unprofessionally hostile all the way through, including prior to the event. The frequency at which she swears ends up making any expletives in the document at all have little impact. Apart from them, all the characters sorta bled together and I couldn't really tell who was saying what without constantly checking. Petowski distinguishes themself a little, but the other three are near-identical.

The narrative prior to the reveal isn't especially interesting. The ship breaks and people go to explore, before coming back and wasting away. Despite the fact that this is obviously a terrible thing, the fact that one of the characters affected was unpleasant and the others were unmemorable didn't cause me to emphasise with them that much.

The actual reveal is fairly unique, and would be something I can appreciate. However, I've got two major issues with it. The first has come up a little earlier in the discussion, and I also agree that it's a pretty big leap in logic to claim that this is some sort of purposefully created dimension just because of its uniqueness. The second is that I find it really hard to believe that they didn't notice anything backwards before the writing. I imagine there'd be visible words somewhere around them before that, and people are pretty far off from being symmetrical too (enough that a reflected one would look instantly very off).

Due to those issues, and the fact that I wasn't especially interested by the buildup to the reveal, I'm downvoting this article.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this chilled my bones once and it made me feel, so good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't now, i'm getting lukewarm vibes from this. The last part just doesn't do it for me. I liked the characters, and I liked the anomaly. The science aspect of it works well too. It just all falls a bit flat. Either way, +1 for effort, the science of it all is a huge highlight for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you clearly put a lot of work into this. I enjoyed the 2nd page a lot, I liked the characters. that said, this doesn't punch me in the gut with shock/awe/discomfort or a sense of mystery like I'd expect from what ultimately becomes the 5000 entry. I didn't get a strong "mystery" vibe from this. I'll probably upvote after the contest, though; I still think it belongs here, just not in the 5000 slot. great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, there actually was a TNG episode called Ship in a Bottle. Good one, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Felt like an episode of Star Trek, loved it!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I haven't heard anything positive about that note yet, so it'll probably end up on the chopping block. It's mostly in there so that this has more going for it in the "mystery" department. It sounds like you dislike the whole idea of it, but I'm hoping that there's something else I can do in the same vein that'll be more effective. Need some more opinions before I decide.

Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a pleasure to read, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[+] Spoilers
– hide block

Hm. I really don't know how I feel about this. I was really digging this and it was a supremely easy +1 right up until the "Archived Correspondence in Regards to SCP-5000". And then it just kinda, came crashing down? I feel like it was a real bad idea to put a note indicating how incompetently/irrationally everyone behaved during the story into the article because it kinda, shattered my suspension of disbelief a little?

The belief this was designed specifically as a puzzle/trap is…weird here too. It feels, a little too thrown out there with not quite enough in the narrative to justify that angle, and also a little too vague: there's not really anything here to really get a feel for what the consequences of it are?

In the end, I guess I'm no-voting: I like the start but when it actually changes to the SCP document, it kinda, falls apart too much for me to upvote :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit, I was slightly terrified when I saw the title for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thanks for pointing that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always a sucker for space SCPs, and this did not disappoint! I'm not 100% sure it necessarily deserves to be 5000, but I'd say it definitely earns a place among Series VI!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's an XXXX in the fourth offset

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
FIRST SCIP! WOOOO!

I'm extremely grateful to everyone who encouraged me to write for the site, everyone who read this and especially my coauthor Habitual_Snubnose for keeping me motivated. This ain't perfect, but I'm really proud of it and I couldn't have made it on my own.

All official credits are under the info bar in the main article.
